gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/68)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/68)
text: إِنَّ الْمُزْدَلِفَةَ أَكْثَرُ بِلَادِ اللَّهِ هَوَامّاً فَإِذَا كَانَتْ لَيْلَةُ التَّرْوِيَةِ نَادَى مُنَادٍ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ يَا مَعْشَرَ الْهَوَامِّ ارْحَلْنَ عَنْ وَفْدِ اللَّهِ
قَالَ فَتَخْرُجُ فِي الْجِبَالِ فَتَسَعُهَا حَيْثُ لَا تُرَى فَإِذَا انْصَرَفَ الْحَاجُّ عَادَتْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Safwan, or a man, from Safwan, from Ibn Bukeyr, from his father,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Al-Muzdalifa is a city of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> with the most insects. So when it is the night of Al-Tarwiyya, a Caller Calls out from the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>: ‘O group of insects! Go away from the delegations of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’
He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So they go out to be in the mountains and they crawl around where they cannot be seen. So when the Pilgims leave, they return’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Safwan or a man from Safwan from ibn Bukayr from his father who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Al-Muzdalifah had more insects than other lands of Allah. On the night of al-Tarwiyah, it was announced from Allah, ‘O insects, move away from where delegations to Allah are about to pass.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘The insects move in the hills in a way that they are not seen. When al-Hajj (pilgrims) return, they come back.’”
